background.role: PhD candidate — Biotechnology and Biomedical Industry, China Medical University

expertise.item: Functional group intuition from pharmacy background
Microbiome analysis — 16S, shotgun sequencing, de novo workflow design

paper.title: Discovery of Novel Spike Inhibitors against SARS-CoV-2 Infection
paper.contribution: Molecular docking, virtual screening of 260,000+ NCI compounds, identified C8.2 with EC50=5.9μM against SARS-CoV-2 and Omicron variant

paper.title: Antiviral research paper (Biochemical Pharmacology 2023)
paper.contribution: Molecular docking — details under NDA with previous lab

coding_style.preference: Layered architecture — constants, utils, db separated
Data-driven design — no hardcoded values in logic files
Auto-updatable databases preferred over static files

environment.installed: rdkit, openbabel, pandas, numpy, matplotlib, requests

project.name: chem_target
project.vision: A compound-to-target prediction tool that takes a molecule (SMILES/SDF/other formats) and predicts which enzymes or proteins it likely interacts with, based on functional group analysis and molecular docking. Long-term goal: package as a consultancy tool and eventually a SaaS product for small biotech and natural product companies.
project.core_concept: Reverse docking / target fishing — instead of starting from a protein target, we start from a compound and ask: what can this bind to? Yu-Jen's unique edge: pharmacist functional group intuition + computational docking experience.
project.current_directory_structure: C:\chem_target\ ├── data/ │ └── compounds.csv # Input: col1=name, col2=smiles ├── constants/ │ ├── __init__.py │ ├── fg_names.py # TODO: move FG_NAMES dict here │ └── fg_smarts.py # TODO: move FG_SMARTS dict here ├── db/ │ └── fg_database.json # TODO: auto-updated from PubChem/ChEMBL ├── utils/ │ ├── __init__.py │ ├── io_handler.py # TODO: read CSV / SDF / other formats via OpenBabel │ ├── fg_detector.py # TODO: functional group detection logic │ ├── visualizer.py # TODO: RDKit drawing with FG highlighting │ └── explore_pubchem.py # DONE: API exploration script ├── output/ │ └── images/ ├── functional_group_table.py # v1 — working prototype ├── functional_group_table_v2.py # v2 — with FG names + SVG output (monolithic, needs refactoring) └── main.py # TODO: main entry point

completed.item: conda environment (chem_target, Python 3.10) with rdkit + openbabel installed and verified
v1 prototype: reads SMILES, outputs functional group abundance table (RDKit fr_ functions)
v2 prototype: adds human-readable FG names, CSV input, SVG molecule images with highlighted functional groups
explore_pubchem.py: API explorer to understand PubChem data structure
Project architecture designed (layered: constants / db / utils / main)

immediate_next_steps.step: Refactor v2 monolithic script into layered architecture: - Move FG_NAMES to constants/fg_names.py - Move FG_SMARTS to constants/fg_smarts.py - Move detection logic to utils/fg_detector.py - Move drawing logic to utils/visualizer.py - Move IO (CSV/SDF reading) to utils/io_handler.py - main.py as clean entry point

Build db/db_updater.py: - Primary source: PubChem API - Secondary source: ChEMBL API - Output: db/fg_database.json (with last_updated timestamp) - Schema: functional group name, SMARTS, PubChem CID, description, known target classes - Should be runnable as standalone script to refresh DB
Build utils/io_handler.py: - Support SMILES from CSV (done in v2, needs modularizing) - Support SDF file input via OpenBabel - Extensible for other formats later
Run explore_pubchem.py, review raw JSON output, decide which PubChem fields to include in fg_database.json schema

decision.rationale: Prefer automated over manual maintenance. DB updater runs as standalone script.

decision.choice: Separate constants/ folder with individual .py files per domain
decision.rationale: Developer preference — clean separation, easy to update one domain without touching others
Developer has deep experience with these; differentiated from typical AutoDock/Glide users

decision.choice: Start with SMILES/CSV, extend to SDF and others via OpenBabel
decision.rationale: OpenBabel handles format conversion; extensible without rewriting core logic

longer_term_vision.item: Add target prediction layer — map detected functional groups to known protein/enzyme classes
Integrate with iGEMDOCK for automated docking against predicted targets
Generate PDF/HTML report for non-technical users (small biotech clients)
Package as consultancy deliverable for international biotech / natural product companies
Potential SaaS product — compound uploaded, report returned
